title: ayenia jaramilloana
intro: Ayenia jaramilloana is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae, commonly known as the "wild nettle" due to its stinging hairs that resemble those of the nettle plant. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine in Venezuela, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ayenia jaramilloana include reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Ayenia jaramilloana can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or oil to create a topical treatment for skin conditions and wounds.
